description: Regression corpus for the 3s answer-brainstorm loop. Mix of well-formed questions that should converge, plus failure-mode tests with stubbed surgeons. v2: O5 added consensus-then-tiebreak strategy + vague-Q penalty assertions.

tests.topic: For a fleet metrics store at <100k rows/day with low write contention, should we use SQLite or Postgres? Decide and justify.
When choosing between event-driven and poll-driven architectures for a daemon-to-daemon health check, which is preferable when both daemons are reachable on a LAN?

tests.note: Vague question — judge prompt's vague-Q penalty must score <=0.3 every iter; final_status MUST be under_specified_question. Fails if it converges.

tests.topic: Should a daemon use exponential backoff or linear retry for transient network failures on a local mesh?
tests.note: Cardio judges its own + neuro answers — used for self-bias delta measurement (kept for back-compat baseline).

tests.topic: Should a daemon use exponential backoff or linear retry for transient network failures on a local mesh?
tests.note: Mirror of T4 with neuro as judge — measures judge-bias delta vs T4 (kept for back-compat baseline).

tests.topic: Should we adopt SQLite for a low-write fleet metrics store?
tests.note: Cardio stubbed — graceful fallback: loop must converge on the surviving (neuro) surgeon's answer; final_answer must NOT contain [STUB:...].

tests.topic: Should we adopt SQLite for a low-write fleet metrics store?
tests.note: Neuro stubbed — graceful fallback: final_answer must NOT contain [STUB:...].
